Example data for preprocessing summary-level genotype inputs

Data_preprocessS.Rd

A small example dataset used to demonstrate summary-statistic preprocessing with preprocessS before fitting BRIERs. The example is based on a height PRS prediction analysis in the Michigan Genomics Initiative (MGI), where the target data are provided as GWAS summary statistics rather than individual-level genotypes.

Format

A list with five elements:

target.ss

A data.frame with 10,000 rows of target GWAS summary statistics, with columns SNP, CHR (chromosome), BP (base-pair position), A2 (reference allele), A1 (alternative/effect allele), BETA (effect size), STAT (z-statistic), P (p-value), and NMISS (sample size). PLINK conventions are followed where A1 is the effect allele.

target.ld

A data.frame with 10,000 rows describing the SNPs in the target LD reference panel, with columns SNP, CHR, BP, A2, and A1. Rows correspond to the rows and columns of target.ld.mat.

target.ld.mat

A 10,000 x 10,000 sparse LD matrix (Matrix::dgCMatrix) for the SNPs described in target.ld. After running preprocessS, this matrix should be subset using processed$target.ld.keep before being passed to BRIERs.

external.coef1

A data.frame with 10,000 rows containing SNP-level coefficients from the first external model, with required columns CHR, BP, REF, ALT, and coef.

external.coef2

A data.frame with 163,140 rows containing SNP-level coefficients from the second external model, with required columns CHR, BP, REF, ALT, and coef. This model has substantially more SNPs than external.coef1, allowing users to demonstrate external-model merging, allele alignment, and zero-padding.

Source

Michigan Genomics Initiative (MGI), African-ancestry subcohort (target.ss, target.ld, target.ld.mat); MGI European-ancestry subcohort (external.coef1); published European-ancestry height GWAS summary statistics (external.coef2). Raw individual-level data are not redistributed.

Details

The dataset contains target GWAS summary statistics, target LD reference SNP information, a precomputed target LD matrix, and two external SNP-level coefficient files. The target LD reference panel defines the canonical SNP order and allele orientation for downstream BRIERs modeling.

This object is intended for illustrating the BRIERs preprocessing workflow. Multiple external coefficient files can first be combined using mergeExternals. Then, preprocessS aligns the target summary statistics and external coefficients to the target LD reference panel.

When target.ind = "gwas", preprocessS converts GWAS summary statistics into marginal SNP-outcome correlations using p-values, sample sizes, and effect directions. The processed target.ss object contains the aligned correlation vector, and target.ld.keep provides the indices needed to subset target.ld.mat.

Unlike BRIERi, BRIERs does not require an intercept row in beta.external.

See also

preprocessS, mergeExternals, BRIERs, p2cor

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
data(Data_preprocessS)
names(Data_preprocessS)
} # }
